era_context: Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty VI, Pepi II), Akkadian Empire (Naram-Sin), mature Harappan civilization. This year continues the pattern of imperial consolidation and urban stability across multiple civilizations. All three major powers are within a century of significant transitions or collapses.

events.title: Pepi II's reign continues; Old Kingdom stability
events.description: Pepi II's reign continues. The Old Kingdom administrative system remains functional, though provincial autonomy is increasing. Pyramid construction and religious activities continue. No major disruptions are recorded for this year.
events.certainty_note: No specific events are recorded for this year. The description reflects the general character of Pepi II's reign.

events.title: Naram-Sin's Akkadian Empire maintains control
events.description: Naram-Sin continues to rule the Akkadian Empire. The empire remains at its territorial zenith, controlling vast territories from the Persian Gulf to Anatolia. Administrative and military structures maintain imperial control. No major disruptions are recorded for this year.

sources.name: Royal inscriptions from the Akkadian period

events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-attested, but specific events for individual years are rarely recorded.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: This year is undifferentiated in the historical record. No specific events are recorded. The entry describes the general character of the era based on broader historical patterns.
